---
title: "About the EVE 113 R Bootcamp"
---

![](images/clipboard-590625097.png)

This R bootcamp resource was initially created and designed in June 2026 to prep for the second summer session of the undergraduate ecology and evolution Coastal Marine Microbes (EVE 113) course taught by Dr. Anya Brown at Bodega Marine Lab (extension of University of California, Davis). All information herein was written and developed with this course in mind by PhD student and Teaching Assistant Tallulah Story.

However, this guide can be widely applicable to any undergraduate or graduate student looking to learn R for ecology! It starts with the basics then slowly builds upon that knowledge, so it can be flexible for any knowledge level (beyond anything too advanced). This might also be a good refresher for those just getting back into `R` after a while away from the application. Either way, no matter how you (dear viewer) may be accessing this tutorial, please feel free to make full use of it in any way that best suits your needs.

For instructors looking to adapt this material for their own courses, please do so! You may credit: **Story and Brown, R Bootcamp 2026**.

If you have questions, concerns, or would like to see changes made to this resource, please contact:

Tallulah Story, tstory\@ucdavis.edu
